content.problemStatement:
Value of an array AAA of length LLL is defined as the sum of (Ai⊕i)(A_i \oplus i)(Ai​⊕i) for all 0≤i<L0 \leq i \lt L0≤i<L, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise xor operation. **Note** that array indices start from zero.

You are given an integer NNN and an array AAA consisting of 2N2^N2N integers where Ai=iA_i = iAi​=i for all 0≤i<2N0 \leq i \lt 2^N0≤i<2N.

**Example :**


For N=1N = 1N=1, you have an array AAA of length 21=22^1 = 221=2 and A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1].
For N=2N = 2N=2, you have an array AAA of length 22=42^2 = 422=4 and A=[0,1,2,3]A = [0, 1, 2, 3]A=[0,1,2,3].
You can do at most KKK operations on this array. In one operation, you can choose two indices iii and jjj (0≤i,j<2N0 \leq i, j \lt 2^N0≤i,j<2N) and swap AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ (i.e. AiA_iAi​ becomes AjA_jAj​ and vice versa).

What is the maximum value of array AAA you can obtain after at most KKK operations?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains a single line of input, two integers N,KN, KN,K.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the maximum value of array after doing at most KKK operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, for N=2N = 2N=2, you have the array A=[0,1,2,3]A = [0, 1, 2, 3]A=[0,1,2,3]. No swap operation is
allowed hence value of array A=(0⊕0)+(1⊕1)+(2⊕2)+(3⊕3)=0+0+0+0=0A = (0 \oplus 0) + (1 \oplus 1) + (2 \oplus 2) + (3 \oplus 3) = 0 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 0A=(0⊕0)+(1⊕1)+(2⊕2)+(3⊕3)=0+0+0+0=0.
In the second test case, initially the array A=[0,1,2,3]A = [0, 1, 2, 3]A=[0,1,2,3]. If you swap A1A_1A1​ and A2A_2A2​, AAA becomes [0,2,1,3][0, 2, 1, 3][0,2,1,3]. Now value of array A=(0⊕0)+(2⊕1)+(1⊕2)+(3⊕3)=0+3+3+0=6A = (0 \oplus 0) + (2 \oplus 1) + (1 \oplus 2) + (3 \oplus 3) = 0 + 3 + 3 + 0 = 6A=(0⊕0)+(2⊕1)+(1⊕2)+(3⊕3)=0+3+3+0=6. There is no possible way such that value of array AAA becomes greater than 666 using one swap operation.
